text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

anyOf.description: Resolve to the centre of the matched element at dispatch time.
Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Touch down at the resolved target. A pointer sequence must open with `down`.

Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Drag the active pointer to a new target. Duration per move comes from `moveDurationMs` on the enclosing `pointer` step.
Hold the current pointer position for `ms` milliseconds. Used to produce long-press and dwell gestures.
Release the pointer. A pointer sequence must close with `up`.

PointerAction.description: One primitive in a pointer sequence. W3C Actions semantics.
